page: The Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is an x86_64 architecture cloud server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and SHA extensions. System memory is configured at 16.0 GB, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 240 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside average multi-core and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Most other metrics, including PassMark CPU and memory scores, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, fall within the average range. Large-model LLM inference is limited, with prompt processing for a 7B model scoring in the bottom 10%.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for general-purpose workloads that require moderate memory and fast local NVMe storage but do not demand sustained, dedicated CPU performance. The hardware profile and benchmark results make it suitable for static web serving, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose application hosting. It is less suitable for multi-threaded CPU-heavy tasks or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose cloud server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded compression tasks, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database transactions perform at average levels. Due to its shared CPU allocation, the server provides a cost-effective resource profile for workloads that do not require continuous dedicated compute power. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight database hosting, and general-purpose application environments.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose cloud server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 240 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and light databases.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose cloud server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 240 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server combining shared AMD EPYC processors with fast local NVMe storage for everyday web and database workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance
Best suited for static web serving and lightweight databases
